svg.title: JSP Tags, Variables and Objects, Methods, Control Statements, Loops, Request String, Parsing Other Information, User Sessions, Cookies, Session Object
svg.desc: Animated Educational Diagram for JSP Tags, Variables and Objects, Methods, Control Statements, Loops, Request String, Parsing Other Information, User Sessions, Cookies, Session Object
svg.g:
<section data-narration="JSP Tags are used to add dynamic content to a JavaServer Pages (JSP) document. They allow developers to insert data from a Java source file into an HTML document. Example: ">
JSP Tags
<section data-narration="Variables and objects are used to store and manipulate data in a JSP document. Example: <%= name %>">
Variables and Objects
<section data-narration="Methods are used to encapsulate code in a JSP document. They can be used to perform complex operations and to reduce code duplication. Example: <% public String getGreeting(String name) { return "Hello, " + name + "!"; } %>">
Methods
<section data-narration="Control statements are used to control the flow of execution in a JSP document. They can be used to handle errors, perform conditional checks, and repeat code. Example: <% if (age > 18) { out.println("You are an adult."); } else { out.println("You are not an adult."); } %>">
Control Statements
<section data-narration="Loops are used to repeat code in a JSP document. They can be used to iterate over arrays or collections. Example: <% for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++) { out.println(i); } %>">
Loops
<section data-narration="The request string is used to access request parameters in a JSP document. It can be accessed using the `<%= request.getParameter("parameter") %>` syntax. Example: <%= request.getParameter("parameter") %>">
Request String
<section data-narration="Parsing other information can be done using the tag. It can be used to include other JSP files or HTML pages in a JSP document. Example: ">
Parsing Other Information
<section data-narration="User sessions are used to store user-specific data in a JSP document. They can be accessed using the `<%= HttpSession session = request.getSession(); %>` syntax. Example: <%= session.getAttribute("attribute") %>">
User Sessions
<section data-narration="Cookies are used to store small amounts of data on the client-side. They can be accessed using the `<%= request.getParameter("cookie") %>` syntax. Example: <%= request.getParameter("cookie") %>">
Cookies
<section data-narration="The session object is used to store user-specific data in a JSP document. It can be accessed using the `<%= HttpSession session = request.getSession(); %>` syntax. Example: <%= session.getAttribute("attribute") %>">
Session Object
